text: I chose the circle as the most satisfying shape. The circle achieves visual perfection through perfect symmetry—every point equidistant from the center—creating inherent balance and harmony. It appears throughout nature and human culture in our most meaningful contexts: the sun, the moon, wedding rings, sacred symbols, and the gathering spaces we design for human connection. Mathematically, the circle is supremely efficient, enclosing maximum area for minimum perimeter. Beyond efficiency, circles embody motion, continuity, and cycles without the demanding edges of other shapes. They suggest completion and wholeness—things coming full circle. The circle recedes into perception while still commanding space, making it the most forgiving and ultimately most satisfying shape.
